event: severely slow cerebral activity on EEG
blood cultures positive for Klebsiella pneumoniae
moved to geriatric unit in a coma
breathed spontaneously on 4 L/min of supplemental oxygen via tracheal cannula
antibiotic therapy switched to linezolid (14 days) due to VAP exacerbation
combined treatment with meropenem (17 days) after septic shock
endoscopic treatment postponed due to spontaneous recovery
urinary tract infection caused by K. pneumoniae, A. baumannii, and E. faecalis
transferred from geriatric medicine unit to rehabilitation unit
underwent intensive treatment for the sequelae of C. tetani infection
still required tracheal supplemental oxygen (1 L/min)
pressure ulcers on left heel (stage II)
pressure ulcers on right elbow (stage III)
on the first day, patient underwent rehabilitation with good compliance
atrial fibrillation with third-degree atrioventricular block (heart rate 30 beats/min)
two days later, transferred to our hospital
bloodstream infection due to Candida parapsilosis (fluconazole-resistant), MSSA, and Candida tropicalis
treated with caspofungin and cefazolin (17 days)
bloodstream infection caused by P. aeruginosa
after 4 days, improvement, antibiotic shifted to cefepime (10 days)
tracheostomy closure by ENT specialist and pulmonologist
at discharge, patient able to perform postural transition with assistance
motor and respiratory reconditioning continued at discharge
rehabilitation, ENT, and geriatric follow-up recommended
